questions.question:
Choose the best answer.
The extension of a Java file is ______.
A) .jav
B) .class
C) .java
D) .py

Choose the best answer.
What does the statement `System.out.println("SISHYA");` do?
A) Takes input from the user
B) Stops the program
C) Prints the text to the screen
D) Saves a file
questions.answer: C) Prints the text to the screen

questions.question:
Choose the best answer.
Which of the following is an arithmetic operator in Java?
A) &&
B) +
C) =
D) !

Choose the best answer.
Which of the following is NOT an arithmetic operator in Java?
A) %
B) -
C) ||
D) *

Choose the best answer.
Which one of the following can be used to store whole numbers in Java?
A) int
B) byte
C) short
D) All of these

Choose the best answer.
The data "Australia" is an example of which data type?
A) int
B) String
C) double
D) None of these

Choose the best answer.
The operator that gives the remainder when one value is divided by another is ______.
A) >
B) %
C) /
D) --

Answer the following questions.
(i) What is the purpose of the `%` operator in Java?
(ii) What is the purpose of the `/` operator in Java?
questions.answer:
(i) The `%` operator gives the remainder after division.
(ii) The `/` operator performs division and gives the quotient.

questions.question:
Answer the following question.
What is the main difference between a unary operator and a binary operator in Java?
questions.answer: A unary operator works on one operand, whereas a binary operator works on two operands.

questions.question:
Answer the following question.
Explain the difference between `System.out.println()` and `System.out.print()`.
questions.answer: `System.out.println()` prints the output and moves to the next line. `System.out.print()` prints the output on the same line without moving to the next line.

questions.question:
Identify a suitable Java data type for each of the following.
(a) Temperature of Antarctica
(b) First letter of your school name
(c) Number of apples
(d) Your name
questions.answer:
(a) double or float
(b) char
(c) int
(d) String

questions.question:
Look at the expression `7 * 3` and answer the following.
(i) Identify the operator.
(ii) Identify the two operands.
(iii) What value does the expression produce?
questions.answer:
(i) The operator is `*`.
(ii) The operands are 7 and 3.
(iii) The expression produces 21.

questions.question: Write a Java program to store your name, age, class, and section in suitable variables and print them in a single line.

Write a Java program to print the pattern shown below.
* * *
* $ *
Write a Java program to create an arithmetic calculator for performing `+`, `-`, `*`, and `/`.

Rohan bought 2 kg of rice at 45 per kg, 1.5 kg of sugar at 40 per kg, and 3 litres of milk at 30 per litre. The shopkeeper gave a discount of 20 on the total bill. Write a Java program to display the final amount to be paid.
Write a Java program to calculate and display the amount received when a shop sells 25 electric scooters, each costing Rs. 134000.
Write a Java program to initialize 5 random numbers and find their average.
